name: Strawberry Mousse Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 cups whole strawberries, rinsed and hulled
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter and flour the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, beat egg whites and cream tartar to soft peaks. Gradually beat in 1/4 cup sugar, continuing to beat until stiff peaks form.
3. In a separate bowl, beat yolks with remaining sugar until light yellow and thickened. Scrape yolks over the whites, add lemon rind, vanilla, and fold together.
4. Sift flour and salt over the batter, folding gently but thoroughly. Transfer to prepared pan.
5. Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes or until the top springs back when lightly touched. Let cool in the pan on a wire rack. If making ahead, remove from the pan and wrap well for storage at room temperature for up to one day or in the freezer for up to two months.
6. For the mousse: In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin over water; set aside. Rinse, hull, and puree strawberries. Combine puree, sugar, and lemon juice in another saucepan and heat gently just long enough to dissolve the sugar. Remove from heat. Warm the gelatin until clear and syrupy.
7. Stir the warmed gelatin into the strawberry mixture. Transfer to a large bowl and chill to the consistency of raw egg white. Whip cream until stiff; fold it into the mousse and return to the fridge.
8. For the syrup: In a small saucepan, bring water and sugar to a boil. Remove from heat, cool, and stir in liqueur.
9. Assemble: Using a long serrated knife, cut the cake into 2 thin layers. Place the top half with the side up in the bottom of a clean 9-inch springform pan. Drizzle half the syrup evenly over the cut sides of each layer.
10. Spoon strawberry mousse over the cake; don't worry if it extends over the sides. Set the remaining layer, cut side over the mousse, pressing gently. Cover and chill thoroughly, overnight if possible.
11. To serve: Release the sides of the pan and transfer the cake to a service plate using a wide lifter. Rinse and hull strawberries, then slice thinly. Whip cream until stiff and firm, sweeten with sugar, and spread evenly over the top and sides of the cake.
12. Arrange berry slices in a circle around the top and bottom edges of the cake. Refrigerate until serving time, which should be within an hour or two.
